title: 10 best herbal teas for grey hair
intro_desc: Herbal teas for grey hair are infusions made from various plants and herbs that are believed to help maintain natural hair color and prevent premature graying. These teas typically contain high levels of antioxidants, vitamins, and minerals that nourish the hair follicles and promote healthy hair growth. Examples of herbal teas that are commonly used for gray hair include rooibos, sage, rosemary, and nettle. By consuming these teas regularly, individuals may experience a reduction in the appearance of gray hairs and an improvement in the overall health and shine of their hair. This can lead to increased confidence and self-esteem, as well as a decreased need for harsh chemical treatments that can damage the hair.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Lemon verbena teas helps with grey hair because it is rich in antioxidants and flavonoids that promote healthy hair growth by nourishing the scalp and follicles. The tea's anti-inflammatory properties also help to reduce oxidative stress, a common factor contributing to premature greying of hair. Additionally, lemon verbena contains a compound called citral, which has been shown to stimulate melanin production in the hair, leading to a reduction in grey hair appearance.
remedy_properties: Antioxidant properties: Lemon verbena tea's antioxidants help protect the hair follicles from damage caused by free radicals, which can contribute to premature graying.
Anti-inflammatory properties: The anti-inflammatory compounds in lemon verbena tea may help reduce inflammation in the scalp, which has been linked to grey hair and other hair loss conditions.
Astringent properties: Lemon verbena's astringent nature can help tighten and strengthen hair follicles, promoting healthy hair growth and potentially reducing the appearance of grey hair.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the lemon verbena plant are used to make medicinal teas as they contain a high concentration of antioxidants and flavonoids, which help to promote hair growth and reduce graying.
Bark: The bark of the lemon verbena plant is used to make medicinal teas as it contains compounds that help to improve circulation to the scalp, promoting healthy hair growth and reducing grey hair.
remedy_recipe: Measure out one teaspoon of dried lemon verbena leaves or two teaspoons of fresh lemon verbena leaves into a heat-resistant cup.
Boil one cup of water in a kettle or on the stove and pour it over the measured lemon verbena leaves.
Allow the mixture to steep for five to seven minutes, depending on desired tea strength and flavor profile.
Strain the tea leaves from the liquid using a tea infuser or a piece of cheesecloth, and discard the solids.
Enjoy your freshly brewed herbal lemon verbena tea immediately, or store it in the refrigerator for up to 24 hours prior to consumption.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Brahmi teas helps with grey hair because it contains antioxidants and adaptogenic compounds that promote overall health and wellness. The herbal tea stimulates blood flow to the scalp, nourishing the hair follicles and promoting a healthy coloration. Additionally, brahmi's adaptogenic properties help to reduce stress and anxiety, which are common contributors to premature graying. By drinking brahmi tea regularly, individuals may experience a reduction in grey hair growth and an improvement in overall hair health and appearance.
remedy_properties: Adaptogenic properties: Brahmi tea's adaptogenic properties help to reduce stress and anxiety, which are known contributors to premature grey hair by promoting relaxation and improving overall well-being.
Antioxidant activity: The antioxidants present in Brahmi tea help to protect the hair follicles from damage caused by free radicals, reducing oxidative stress that can lead to grey hair.
Enhanced circulation: Brahmi tea's vasodilatory effects promote increased blood flow to the scalp, which helps to nourish and revitalize hair growth, potentially reversing or slowing down graying.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots are used to treat premature graying as they contain antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds that help promote healthy hair growth.
Leaves: The leaves are rich in vitamins and minerals that nourish the hair follicles, promoting thicker, darker hair and reducing grey hair.
remedy_recipe: Measure one teaspoon of dried bacopa monnieri powder into a small bowl.
Heat one cup of filtered water in a medium saucepan over high heat for five minutes.
Steep the bacopa monnieri powder in the hot water for ten to fifteen minutes.
Strain the tea mixture through a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth into a large mug.
Allow the tea to cool to a comfortable drinking temperature before consuming.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Pot marigold teas helps with grey hair because it contains antioxidants and flavonoids that promote healthy hair growth and color. The tea's anti-inflammatory properties also help to soothe the scalp, reducing irritation and promoting a healthy environment for melanin production. Additionally, pot marigold's ability to stimulate blood flow may help to deliver oxygen and nutrients to the hair follicles, contributing to thicker, darker locks that are less prone to graying.
remedy_properties: Antioxidants: Pot marigold tea is rich in antioxidants, which help to neutralize free radicals that can damage hair follicles and lead to premature graying.
Vitamin C: The high vitamin C content in pot marigold tea helps to promote collagen production, which can improve the strength and elasticity of hair, reducing the appearance of grey strands.
Chlorophyll: Chlorophyll present in pot marigold tea has been shown to help restore the natural pigmentation of hair, promoting a healthy, darker color.
remedy_parts: Roots: Potent in antioxidants and flavonoids, which help to promote melanin production and reduce oxidative stress associated with grey hair.
Seeds: Rich in fatty acids and antioxidants, which nourish and strengthen the hair follicles, promoting a healthy and vibrant appearance.
remedy_recipe: Measure out one tablespoon of dried calendula flowers for every eight ounces of boiling water needed.
Heat the water in a teapot or kettle until it reaches a rolling boil and let it simmer for five minutes.
Add the measured calendula flowers to the hot water and allow them to steep for ten to fifteen minutes.
Strain the tea into a cup using a fine-mesh strainer or cheesecloth to remove the solids.
Serve the tea immediately, or refrigerate it for up to 24 hours if desired.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Cornflower teas helps with grey hair because it is rich in antioxidants, which can help to nourish and strengthen the hair follicles. The tea's ant-inflammatory properties may also reduce oxidative stress on the scalp, which can contribute to premature graying. Additionally, cornflower contains flavonoids, which have been shown to stimulate melanin production, potentially helping to restore natural hair color. By drinking this tea regularly, individuals may experience a reduction in grey hair and an overall improvement in their hair's health and appearance.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory property: Herbal cornflower tea has anti-inflammatory compounds that may help reduce inflammation in the scalp, which can contribute to premature graying by damaging hair follicles.

Antioxidant property: Cornflower tea's antioxidants neutralize free radicals that can damage hair pigmentation, promoting healthier and more vibrant hair color, including reducing the appearance of grey hair.

Cytotoxicity and anti-fungal properties: Cornflower's compounds have been shown to inhibit the growth of certain fungi and bacteria that can cause scalp irritation and lead to premature graying or hair loss.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in saponins and flavonoids, which help to nourish and strengthen hair follicles, promoting healthy hair growth and reversing grey hair.
Seeds: High in omega-3 fatty acids and antioxidants, which moisturize and protect the scalp, promoting a healthy environment for hair growth and reducing grey hair.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh or dried cornflower petals and leaves according to local regulations and guidelines.
Measure out one teaspoon of dried cornflower petals for every eight ounces of boiling water.
Combine the measured cornflower with boiling water in a teapot or infuser and steep for seven to ten minutes.
Strain the tea into a cup using a tea strainer or cheesecloth and discard the solids immediately.
Allow the tea to cool for a few minutes before serving hot or chilled depending on personal preference.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Horsetail teas helps with grey hair because it is rich in silica, a mineral that promotes healthy hair growth by strengthening the bonds between keratin cells. Silica also improves circulation to the scalp, increasing oxygen and nutrient supply to hair follicles, which can help to reverse or slow down premature graying. Additionally, horsetail's antioxidant properties may help protect the hair from damage caused by free radicals, promoting a healthier, shinier appearance.
remedy_properties: Silica content: Horsetail tea is rich in silica, which helps to strengthen hair follicles and promote healthy hair growth, reducing the appearance of grey hair.
Antioxidant properties: The antioxidants present in horsetail tea help to protect the hair follicles from damage caused by free radicals, which can contribute to premature greying of hair.
Vitamins and minerals: Horsetail tea is a rich source of vitamins B and C, as well as minerals like potassium and calcium, which are essential for maintaining healthy hair growth and pigmentation.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in silica, which helps to strengthen and fortify hair follicles, promoting healthy hair growth and reducing the appearance of grey hair.
Rhyzomes: High in antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds, which can help to protect the scalp and promote a healthy environment for hair growth.
remedy_recipe: Measure one teaspoon of dried horsetail leaves and stems for every eight ounces of water.
Heat filtered or bottled water in a teapot or kettle until it reaches a simmer temperature.
Pour the hot water over the measured horsetail mixture and let it steep for 10 to 15 minutes.
Strain the tea into a cup using a fine-mesh strainer or cheesecloth to remove solids.
Drink the herbal tea immediately, or store it in an airtight container for up to 24 hours.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Maidenhair tree teas helps with grey hair because it is rich in antioxidants and flavonoids, which work together to promote healthy hair growth and reduce oxidative stress. The tea's ability to stimulate blood flow to the scalp also helps to nourish the hair follicles, leading to thicker, darker locks. Additionally, maidenhair tree teas has been shown to help regulate melanin production, which can help to slow down or even reverse grey hair.
remedy_properties: Antioxidant properties: The antioxidant compounds present in Maidenhair Tree tea, such as flavonoids and phenolic acids, help protect the hair follicles from oxidative stress and damage caused by free radicals, which can contribute to premature graying.

Anti-inflammatory properties: The anti-inflammatory compounds in Maidenhair Tree tea may help reduce inflammation in the scalp, which has been linked to grey hair development, promoting healthy hair growth and reducing the appearance of grey hairs.

Vitamin and mineral content: Maidenhair Tree tea is rich in vitamins (such as vitamin E) and minerals (like copper), which are essential for maintaining healthy hair pigmentation and promoting melanin production, thereby helping to reduce or reverse grey hair.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds, the roots help to promote blood flow to the scalp, reducing grey hair and promoting a healthy mane.
Bark: Containing a high concentration of flavonoids, the bark helps to strengthen and protect the hair follicles, leading to thicker, healthier locks.
remedy_recipe: Harvest high-quality ginkgo biloba leaves from mature trees, ensuring proper identification and cleanliness.
Rinse the harvested leaves with filtered water to remove impurities, then air-dry them completely for future use.
Combine 15-20 grams of dried maidenhair tree leaves with one liter of boiling distilled water, allowing it steep for 10-15 minutes.
Strain the tea mixture through a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth into a clean container, discarding solids and reserving liquid for consumption.
Allow the brewed tea to cool to room temperature before refrigerating it for up to 24 hours, ready for consumption as needed.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Sea buckthorn teas helps with grey hair because it is rich in antioxidants and fatty acids that nourish and strengthen the hair follicles, promoting healthy hair growth. The tea's potent antioxidant properties also help to protect the hair from damage caused by free radicals, which can contribute to premature graying. Additionally, sea buckthorn's omega-7 fatty acid content helps to improve blood circulation to the scalp, allowing for better oxygenation and nutrient delivery to the hair follicles, leading to a reduction in grey hair appearance.
remedy_properties: Antioxidant properties: Sea buckthorn tea's high antioxidant content helps to combat free radicals that can cause oxidative stress and damage to hair follicles, leading to premature graying.

Vitamin C and E: The vitamin C and E present in sea buckthorn tea promote collagen production and improve blood circulation, which can help to maintain healthy and shiny hair, reducing the appearance of grey hair.

Omega-7 fatty acids: The omega-7 fatty acids found in sea buckthorn oil, often used in tea blends, nourish and moisturize the scalp, promoting a healthy environment for hair growth and helping to reduce grey hair.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in antioxidants and flavonoids, sea buckthorn roots help nourish and regenerate hair follicles, promoting healthy hair growth and reducing grey hair.
Seeds: High in essential fatty acids and vitamins, sea buckthorn seeds provide intense moisturizing and nourishing properties, helping to strengthen and restore the hair's natural color.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh or dried sea buckthorn fruit, hippophae rhamnoides, for 2-3 ounces per serving.
Rinse the fruit thoroughly with cold water and remove any stems or debris.
Combine 2 cups of boiling water with 1 cup of the cleaned sea buckthorn fruit in a large tea infuser.
Steep the mixture for 10-15 minutes, then strain the liquid into a separate container using a cheesecloth or fine-mesh sieve.
Allow the tea to cool and store it in an airtight container in the refrigerator for up to 3 days or freeze for later use.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Humulus lupulus teas helps with grey hair because it contains a unique combination of antioxidants, flavonoids, and lignans that work synergistically to promote healthy hair growth. The tea's active compounds have been shown to nourish the scalp, stimulate blood flow, and reduce oxidative stress, which are all conducive to a fuller, darker hair color. Additionally, humulus lupulus has natural anti-inflammatory properties that can help soothe an itchy scalp and prevent dandruff, creating a healthy environment for hair growth.
remedy_properties: Antioxidant activity: Humulus Lupulus tea contains antioxidants like flavonoids, phenolic acids, and lignans that help protect the hair follicles from oxidative stress and damage, which can contribute to premature graying.
Anti-inflammatory effects: The tea's anti-inflammatory properties may help reduce inflammation in the scalp and promote healthy blood flow, which can improve hair growth and reduce grey hair.

Hormone regulation: Humulus Lupulus tea has been shown to have a regulatory effect on hormone production, including thyroid hormones that play a role in hair pigmentation, which may help promote darker hair color and slow down the process of graying.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of Humulus lupulus contain bioactive compounds like flavonoids and terpenes, which have been shown to promote melanogenesis and reduce oxidative stress, making them a suitable component for tea blends aimed at promoting healthy hair color.

Seeds: The seeds of the plant are rich in fatty acids and antioxidants, which help to nourish the scalp and promote healthy hair growth, while also potentially inhibiting the conversion of melanin to white pigment.
remedy_recipe: Measure accurately one teaspoon of dried humulus lupulus herb per eight ounces of boiling water.
Heat a teapot or teacup by rinsing it with boiling water to prevent any residue contamination.
Steep the measured herb in the heated water for five to seven minutes allowing flavors and active compounds extraction.
Strain the tea using a tea strainer or cheesecloth into a separate container removing the solid herbal particles.
Serve the prepared tea hot or chilled as preferred adding optional sweeteners or flavorings if desired.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Elecampane teas helps with grey hair because it is rich in antioxidants, which help to reduce oxidative stress on the body. This can lead to a slowing down of the graying process by protecting the hair follicles from damage caused by free radicals. Additionally, elecampane has been known to improve circulation and promote healthy skin, which can also contribute to the growth of thicker, darker hair.
remedy_properties: Antioxidant Properties: Elecampane tea contains antioxidants that help protect the hair follicles from damage caused by free radicals, which can contribute to premature graying.

Inflammation Reduction: The anti-inflammatory compounds in elecampane tea may help reduce inflammation in the scalp, which has been linked to grey hair, and promote a healthy environment for hair growth.
Hormone Regulation: Elecampane tea contains flavonoids and terpenes that may help regulate hormonal imbalances, which can contribute to grey hair, particularly in women.
remedy_parts: Roots: Elecampane roots are commonly used in herbal remedies because they contain a high concentration of inulin, which has been shown to promote hair growth and reduce grayness.
Rhyzomes: The rhyzome of the elecampane plant is rich in saponins, which have been traditionally used to darken and strengthen hair, reducing the appearance of gray hair.
remedy_recipe: Harvest high-quality fresh or dried elecampane roots iula helenium and clean them thoroughly.
Measure two to three teaspoons of the cleaned root material for every eight ounces of water.
Combine the measured root material with boiling water in a large pot or tea infuser and steep for ten to fifteen minutes.
Strain the tea through a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth into a large glass container to remove any solids.
Store the prepared tea in an airtight container in the refrigerator for up to three days and consume immediately.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: English lavender teas helps with grey hair because of its calming and balancing properties. Lavender oil, a key component in herbal English lavender teas, has been shown to stimulate blood flow and promote healthy follicles, which can help to reduce graying and promote a natural pigmentation process. Additionally, the relaxing effects of lavender tea may also alleviate stress, a common contributor to premature graying, allowing for a healthier and more vibrant hair color.
remedy_properties: Antioxidants: English lavender tea contains antioxidants that help protect the body from free radical damage, which can contribute to premature graying and thinning of hair.

Anti-inflammatory compounds: Lavender's anti-inflammatory properties may help reduce inflammation in the scalp, which is associated with hair loss and grey hair, allowing for healthier and fuller hair growth.

Soothing and calming effects: The calming and relaxing properties of lavender tea may help reduce stress and anxiety, which are common triggers for hair loss and premature graying, promoting a healthy and balanced environment for hair growth.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in antioxidants and flavonoids, which help promote healthy hair growth and reduce grey hair.
Flowers: Containing apigenin, a flavone that helps to strengthen hair follicles and improve hair color.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 2 tablespoons of dried english lavender buds or 3-4 teaspoons of fresh leaves.
Combine the harvested lavender with 8 ounces of boiling water in a heat-resistant teapot.
Steep for 5-7 minutes allowing the mixture to infuse and release its aromatic compounds.
Strain the tea into a cup using a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth to remove the solids.
Enjoy the soothing and calming english lavender tea, warm or chilled, within 24 hours.

supplementary_best_treatment: The best way to treat grey hair with herbal teas is by rinsing your hair with strong brews of herbal infusions. Chamomile tea brings out natural highlights and adds shine, while sage tea darkens hair and promotes growth. Rooibos tea, rich in antioxidants, helps maintain hair health and can slow down the graying process. Regular use of these herbal teas as a final rinse after washing your hair can result in softer, stronger, and more vibrant locks.
supplementary_causes: Ailments similar to grey hair that are treated with herbal teas include hair loss and damaged hair. For hair loss, teas made from nettle, rosemary, and ginkgo biloba are often recommended due to their high nutrient content and stimulating effects on the scalp. Damaged hair can be treated with tea rinses made from chamomile, sage, and rooibos, which are rich in antioxidants and help to smooth the hair cuticle, add shine, and promote hair growth. These herbal teas can be used as a rinse after shampooing or as a leave-in treatment, depending on the desired result. However, it is important to note that while herbal teas can support hair health, they should not be used as a substitute for medical treatment for serious hair conditions.
